results.description: DELETE endpoint at /api/skills/:plugin/:skill recursively removes skill directory and returns { ok: true }
results.evidence: src/eval-server/api-routes.ts lines 246-263: router.delete handler resolves skill dir, checks existence, checks origin, calls rmSync with { recursive: true, force: true }, returns { ok: true, deleted: ... }

results.description: DELETE endpoint returns 403 for installed (read-only) skills
results.evidence: src/eval-server/api-routes.ts line 252-255: classifyOrigin check returns 403 with correct error message when origin === 'installed'

results.description: DELETE endpoint returns 404 if skill directory does not exist
results.evidence: src/eval-server/api-routes.ts line 248-250: existsSync check returns 404 with 'Skill directory not found' message

results.description: Client-side api.deleteSkill(plugin, skill) method calls DELETE endpoint
results.evidence: src/eval-ui/src/api.ts lines 125-127: deleteSkill method added to api object, calls fetchJson with DELETE method to /api/skills/${plugin}/${skill}

results.description: DetailHeader shows delete button (trash icon) for source skills only, hidden when isReadOnly
results.evidence: src/eval-ui/src/components/DetailHeader.tsx: button wrapped in {!isReadOnly && onDelete && (...)} conditional, renders TrashIcon SVG component

results.description: Clicking delete opens confirmation dialog naming the skill
results.evidence: DetailHeader.tsx onClick handler calls window.confirm(`Delete skill "${skill}"? This cannot be undone.`) before invoking onDelete

results.description: Confirming deletion calls API, refreshes sidebar, clears selection
results.evidence: SkillWorkspace.tsx handleDelete: await api.deleteSkill(state.plugin, state.skill) -> refreshSkills() -> clearSelection(). Error case logs to console without clearing selection.

results.description: Canceling confirmation dialog closes without side effects
results.evidence: DetailHeader.tsx: window.confirm returns false -> onDelete is never called. Standard browser confirm behavior.

results.description: Delete button disabled while benchmark or other operation is running
results.evidence: DetailHeader.tsx: button has disabled={isRunning} where isRunning checks caseRunStates for 'running' or 'queued' status. Also applies opacity 0.4 and cursor not-allowed styling.

findings.title: Path traversal vulnerability in DELETE endpoint via resolveSkillDir
findings.description: The DELETE handler passes user-controlled :plugin and :skill URL params directly to resolveSkillDir() which uses path.join() without sanitization. While the router regex ([^/]+) prevents slashes, '..' is allowed. A request like DELETE /api/skills/../../../etc would resolve to paths outside the project root. resolveSkillDir's fallback return (line 28) returns directPath without existence check. If the traversed path happens to exist, classifyOrigin would classify it as 'source' (since '..' is not in installedPrefixes), and rmSync would delete it recursively. This is the same pattern the static file server guards against (eval-server.ts line 96-105) but the skill API routes lack this guard.
findings.recommendation: Add a path containment check after resolveSkillDir: verify that the resolved skillDir starts with the root path (similar to the static file server pattern). Example: if (!path.resolve(skillDir).startsWith(path.resolve(root))) return sendJson(res, { error: 'Invalid path' }, 400, req);

findings.title: Zero test coverage for all four tasks
findings.description: The spec (tasks.md) requires tests for T-001 (api-routes-delete.test.ts with 4 test cases), T-002 (api.test.ts extension), T-003 (DetailHeader.test.tsx with 5 test cases), and T-004 (SkillWorkspace.delete.test.tsx with 2 test cases). None of these test files exist in the commit. The increment metadata specifies testMode: 'TDD' and coverageTarget: 90. No tests were written at all.
findings.recommendation: Write the test files specified in tasks.md: api-routes-delete.test.ts, extend api.test.ts, DetailHeader.test.tsx, and SkillWorkspace.delete.test.tsx. At minimum, the server endpoint tests (200/403/404/500 responses) and the UI visibility/interaction tests are needed before shipping.

findings.title: handleDelete error handling only logs to console -- no user feedback
findings.description: In SkillWorkspace.tsx, handleDelete catches API errors with console.error('Failed to delete skill:', err) but provides no visual feedback to the user. If the server returns 403 (tried to delete installed skill via API directly) or 500 (filesystem error), the user sees nothing -- the confirmation dialog has already closed and nothing visible happens. The spec (AC-US1-07) says 'calls the API, refreshes the sidebar, and clears the selection' but doesn't specify error handling behavior. However, silent failure is a poor UX pattern.
findings.recommendation: Add a toast/alert for failed deletions so the user knows the operation failed. Even a simple window.alert('Failed to delete skill: ' + err.message) would be better than silent console logging.

findings.title: onDelete always passed to DetailHeader regardless of isReadOnly
findings.description: SkillWorkspace.tsx passes onDelete={handleDelete} unconditionally to DetailHeader. The DetailHeader component correctly gates rendering with {!isReadOnly && onDelete && (...)}, so the button won't appear for read-only skills. However, handleDelete still calls api.deleteSkill regardless -- it doesn't check isReadOnly. If onDelete were somehow invoked programmatically for a read-only skill (e.g., keyboard shortcut, browser extension), the API call would proceed and the server's 403 guard would be the only protection. This is defense-in-depth, not a functional bug, but it's worth noting that the client doesn't short-circuit.
findings.recommendation: Consider conditionally passing onDelete only when !isReadOnly: onDelete={isReadOnly ? undefined : handleDelete}. This is minor since the server already blocks it, but it's cleaner.
